name: LMDB
description: LMDB (Lightning Memory-Mapped Database) is an ultra-fast, compact, embedded key-value store that uses memory-mapped files with a B+Tree design. It provides fully ACID transactions with MVCC (Multi-Version Concurrency Control), allowing concurrent readers to operate without blocking a single writer. As an embedded database with zero-copy reads, LMDB is ideal for applications requiring minimal overhead, high read performance, and predictable latency. Widely used in OpenLDAP, cryptocurrency nodes, and as a storage backend in distributed systems.
format_on_disk: B+Tree memory-mapped (single data file + lock file)
core_strengths: MVCC with concurrent readers that never block a single writer
Zero-copy reads via memory-mapped files, fully exploiting OS buffer cache
ACID transactions with full crash recovery via copy-on-write
Extremely compact — the library is ~40 KB of tightly optimized C
Deterministic read latency with no background compaction threads
best_use_cases: High-concurrency read workloads with occasional writes
Embedded metadata and configuration storage in long-running processes
Local caching layers where RocksDB-level tuning is not needed
remarks: LMDB is single-writer — only one write transaction at a time. Writes must fit entirely in memory (the database is mapped to a fixed virtual address range). The default map size on 64-bit is 1 MB; increase it with map_size before writes exceed it. LMDB does not use a write-ahead log; a crash during a write transaction rolls back to the last committed state automatically. Keys are lexicographically ordered by default, so prefix scans are efficient.
